report_name: Sandworm APT Evolves, Targeting Misconfigured Edge Devices for Direct OT Access

threat_actors.name: Sandworm
threat_actors.description: A highly sophisticated Russian state-sponsored APT group (GRU GTsST) that is shifting its tactics to exploit misconfigured edge devices for direct access to Operational Technology (OT) networks.
threat_actors.goals: To gain direct and rapid access to OT networks for potential disruption, sabotage, or espionage.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: The Sandworm group exploits remote services like VPNs on edge devices to gain initial access to the network perimeter.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Sandworm targets and exploits unpatched vulnerabilities in the firmware of internet-facing edge devices.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor uses default or stolen credentials to log into misconfigured edge devices.

attack_patterns.name: Network Sniffing
attack_patterns.description: Once on a compromised edge device, Sandworm sniffs network traffic to and from the OT network to gather information.

malware.name: Industroyer
malware.description: A destructive malware mentioned as a potential payload Sandworm could deploy in an OT network to cause disruption or sabotage.

malware.name: Triton
malware.description: A destructive malware framework mentioned as a potential payload Sandworm could deploy to manipulate Safety Instrumented System (SIS) controllers.

identities.name: Critical Infrastructure
identities.description: The primary target sector for Sandworm's attacks, which includes Industrial Control Systems (ICS) and Operational Technology (OT) environments.

infrastructure.name: Compromised Edge Devices
infrastructure.description: Misconfigured or vulnerable edge devices, such as firewalls and VPNs, at the IT/OT boundary that are exploited by Sandworm to pivot directly into OT networks.

indicators.name: Anomalous IT to OT Network Traffic
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous IT to OT Network Traffic
id: 2b1a3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects network traffic from the IT network segment to the OT network segment using non-standard or unexpected ports, which could indicate an attacker pivoting from a compromised edge device.
references:
  - https://varutra.com/blog/latest-cyber-security-attack-news-today-cyber-threat-post/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-16
tags:
  - attack.t0886
level: medium
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    src_ip_zone: 'it_network'
    dst_ip_zone: 'ot_network'
  filter:
    dst_port:
      - 102 # Siemens S7
      - 502 # Modbus
      - 2404 # IEC 60870-5-104
      - 20000 # DNP3
      - 44818 # EtherNet/IP
      - 4840 # OPC UA
  condition: selection and not filter
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate, but un-documented, IT-to-OT communication.
  - Misconfigured network zone definitions.
indicators.description: Detects network traffic crossing the IT/OT boundary on non-standard ports, which could indicate lateral movement from a compromised edge device.

indicators.name: Anomalous VPN Login to Edge Device
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous VPN Login to Edge Device
id: 8f7e6d5c-4b3a-4e2d-9c8b-1a2b3c4d5e6f
status: experimental
description: Detects successful VPN logins from unusual geographic locations, which could indicate compromised credentials for an edge device being used by an attacker.
references:
  - https://varutra.com/blog/latest-cyber-security-attack-news-today-cyber-threat-post/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-16
tags:
  - attack.t0859
  - attack.t0886
level: medium
logsource:
  category: authentication
  product: vpn
detection:
  selection:
    action: 'success'
    logon_type: 'vpn'
  filter:
    source_country:
      - 'USA'
      - 'Canada'
      - 'United Kingdom'
      # Add other expected countries for legitimate remote access
  condition: selection and not filter
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate employees traveling or using personal VPNs.
indicators.description: Detects successful VPN logins from unusual geolocations, suggesting credential compromise of an edge device.

indicators.name: Network Device Configuration Discovery
indicators.pattern:
title: Network Device Configuration Discovery Command
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of commands used to display the running configuration of a network device, which could indicate an attacker performing reconnaissance on a compromised edge device.
references:
  - https://varutra.com/blog/latest-cyber-security-attack-news-today-cyber-threat-post/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-16
tags:
  - attack.t1082
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'show running-config'
      - 'show configuration'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity. Correlation with anomalous logins is recommended.
indicators.description: Detects execution of 'show running-config' command, which may indicate an attacker performing reconnaissance on a compromised network device.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous IT to OT Network Traffic
Anomalous VPN Login to Edge Device
